meta.name: AI Orchestrator Agent
meta.description: A meta-agent that manages payments to downstream AI services and data providers. Enforces per-service budgets, requires memo fields for auditability, and blocks any payment to unregistered recipients. Purpose-built for multi-agent economic coordination.
meta.useCase: Parent agent pays child agents for compute, data, and inference — all within strict per-category limits

identity.description: Multi-agent coordinator — routes payments to downstream AI workers within policy

native.note: Primarily for gas; main budget is in USDC

tokens.note: Primary payment token for downstream services

allowed.name: USDC on Base
allowed.description: Native USDC — payment token for downstream agent work

allowed.name: x402 Payment Gateway
allowed.description: HTTP-native micropayments for AI service providers
allowed.note: Enables RFC-style HTTP 402 Payment Required flows

allowed.name: transfer(address,uint256)
allowed.description: USDC transfer to registered agent or service

blocked.name: approve(address,uint256)
blocked.reason: Open-ended approvals not permitted — each payment must be explicit and bounded

recipients.note: Payments only to ERC-8004 registered agents or explicitly whitelisted addresses. Prevents misdirected funds in multi-agent swarms.

session.note: Long-running orchestrator renews automatically within budget. Human reviews monthly.

useCases: Multi-agent pipeline: orchestrator pays Venice for inference, The Graph for data, downstream specialist agents for subtasks — all bounded by per-category daily budgets
AI marketplace client: agent autonomously purchases compute from the cheapest registered ERC-8004 providers, capped at $35/day
Research agent: pays data APIs and inference services for automated research tasks, with full audit trail per job ID
Composable with x402: agent receives HTTP 402 responses and pays inline for API access — no human needed, no open approval

composability.note: This policy is designed to nest. The AI Orchestrator itself can be a downstream recipient of a higher-level orchestrator, with its own bounded budget. AgentScope policies compose recursively — each layer enforced by separate on-chain module.
